talk_metadata.talk_title: Molecular glues to induce cancer cell death
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we only see activity in models where BCL6 is high"
Speaker said: "the hook effect can be deleterious if the gain-of-function requires continual exposure"
Speaker said: "we call them the kiss of death experiments, transient exposure of the cells will send the signal in that direction"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Requires high BCL6 expression; low or negative BCL6 tumors show no response
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: only see activity in models where BCL6 is high

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Relies on fractional occupancy; low‑affinity binders may limit potency and therapeutic window
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Broad enrollment without BCL6 biomarker stratification could dilute signal

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed ChIP‑seq data that BRD4 is relocalized to BCL6 loci, yet concluded that gain‑of‑function glues are universally superior to loss‑of‑function approaches without addressing context‑specific dependency on BCL6 expression

key_data_gaps: Lack of systematic biomarker‑driven patient selection

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop a diagnostic assay for BCL6 expression to select patients who will respond to TSIP glues
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we only see activity in models where BCL6 is high"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate transcriptomic and proteomic data to define a BCL6‑high signature for patient enrichment

crispro_opportunity.description: Combine TSIP glues with agents that up‑regulate BCL6 or enhance BRD4 recruitment to broaden efficacy
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker discussed fractional occupancy and the possibility of boosting the ternary complex plateau
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model ternary complex kinetics to predict synergistic partners that increase occupancy

cited_competitors.name: Boehringer Ingelheim
cited_competitors.context: provided BTB domain binders used as starting points for BCL6 glues

cited_competitors.name: Bristol Myers Squibb
cited_competitors.context: provided CDK9 binder used in alternative TSIP constructs

unresolved_questions: What is the long‑term safety of sub‑stoichiometric gain‑of‑function glues in normal tissues?
Can the hook effect be mitigated through formulation or dosing strategies?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Chemical and genetic convergence of molecular glues targeting KBDBD4
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the cancer insertion and the small molecule actually exactly mimic one another"
Speaker said: "it is one of the first examples of a gain‑of‑function mutation that can cause the recruitment and degradation of a new substrate"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Relies on HDAC active‑site engagement; HDAC inhibitors can block the glue effect

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Binding pocket is shallow and mimics a simple amino‑acid insertion, suggesting ease of resistance via point mutations
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Dependence on HDAC active‑site occupancy may limit selectivity and allow competition with HDAC inhibitors

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented structural data showing UM171 occupies the HDAC active site, yet concluded that small‑molecule design could be prospective despite the interface being highly specific and seemingly not druggable

key_data_gaps: No in‑vivo efficacy data for UM171 or mutant KBDBD4 glues
Unclear therapeutic index for HDAC‑dependent degradation

crispro_opportunity.description: Use CrisPRO to design small‑molecule mimetics of the 2B2C loop insertions that can recapitulate the gain‑of‑function without HDAC competition
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "the cancer insertion and the small molecule actually exactly mimic one another"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model protein‑protein interface hotspots and propose chemotypes that occupy the same pocket as the inserted residues

crispro_opportunity.description: Leverage deep mutational scanning data to identify other glueable hotspots on E3 ligases beyond KBDBD4
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker described a DMS library covering >5,000 mutations that identified many gain‑of‑function variants
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate DMS data to predict which surface patches are amenable to small‑molecule bridging

unresolved_questions: Can a small molecule fully replace the cancer insertion to achieve comparable degradation potency?
What are the off‑target effects of broadly activating KBDBD4 in normal cells?

talk_metadata.talk_title: TRIM21 molecular glues targeting the nuclear pore complex
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "TRIM21 expression strongly predicted sensitivity"
Speaker said: "the compounds directly bind TRIM21, which is then recruited to Nup98"
Speaker said: "multiple components of the nuclear pore complex were ubiquitinated and degraded in a TRIM21‑dependent manner"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Potential impact on normal cells due to global nuclear pore degradation; therapeutic index not fully defined
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: TRIM21‑high cell lines are sensitive, TRIM21‑low are resistant
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: Selectivity gap between cancer and immortalized normal cells (23‑fold shift reported)

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Induce TRIM21‑mediated degradation of nuclear pore complex
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: No biomarker‑driven patient selection; reliance on TRIM21 expression not prospectively validated
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: TRIM21 protein level or activity assay
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Early phase without clear dose‑response window for nuclear transport inhibition

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Binding pocket is shallow; many chemically diverse glues can engage, suggesting limited IP moat
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on TRIM21 expression limits patient population; potential off‑target degradation of other multimeric substrates

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that TRIM21 glues cause broad NPC degradation yet concluded that the approach will have a favorable therapeutic index without fully addressing potential toxicity to normal cells

key_data_gaps: Long‑term safety of nuclear pore degradation in vivo

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop a CrisPRO‑driven expression signature to identify tumors with high TRIM21 activity and NPC dependency
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "TRIM21 expression strongly predicted sensitivity"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Integrate transcriptomic and proteomic data to build a predictive model for TRIM21‑glue responsiveness

crispro_opportunity.description: Combine TRIM21 glues with agents that sensitize nuclear transport pathways to lower required dose and improve therapeutic index
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker discussed activation lethality and correlation with cell‑kill readouts
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate combinatorial effects on NPC stability and predict synergistic partners

cited_competitors.name: Erastin
cited_competitors.context: Original ferroptosis inducer that was chemically optimized into PRLX series

cited_competitors.name: Complex I inhibitors
cited_competitors.context: Mentioned as having similar toxicity profile to nuclear transport inhibition

unresolved_questions: What is the therapeutic window for NPC degradation in normal versus cancer tissues?
Can alternative E3 ligases be recruited to achieve NPC degradation with a better safety profile?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Induced‑proximity degradation of the druggable proteome
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we created an active degrader molecular glue, JWJ‑0136, with ~80% Dmax"
Speaker said: "ZBTB11 degradation normalizes oxidative phosphorylation in KRAS‑inhibitor‑resistant cells"
Speaker said: "our ZBTB11 degraders have no significant effects on neuronal health"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Limited degradation of close homologs (ZBTB21, ZFP91, WIZ) may cause off‑target effects in certain contexts
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Degradation of ZBTB11 rescues KRAS‑inhibitor resistance in a subset of pancreatic models

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Relies on well‑characterized CRBN pocket; many groups can generate similar chemotypes, limiting IP moat
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Low oral bioavailability of early hits; need scaffold hopping for drug‑like properties

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker demonstrated that ZBTB11 degradation reverses metabolic reprogramming yet also noted that ZBTB11 is low‑abundance and understudied, raising a contradiction between target tractability and therapeutic relevance

key_data_gaps: In‑vivo pharmacokinetics and efficacy of optimized ZBTB11 degraders

crispro_opportunity.description: Apply CrisPRO to predict cereblon‑compatible glues for other zinc‑finger transcription factors involved in metabolic resistance
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted the C2H2 zinc‑finger motif as analogous to IMiD‑targeted TFs
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model degron‑compatible surfaces across the zinc‑finger family to prioritize new targets

crispro_opportunity.description: Incorporate ZBTB11 degradation readouts as pharmacodynamic biomarkers in KRAS‑inhibitor combination trials
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker showed that ZBTB11 degradation normalizes oxidative phosphorylation and restores KRAS‑inhibitor sensitivity
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate metabolomic signatures to define early PD markers for combination regimens

cited_competitors.name: IMiD class drugs (e.g., lenalidomide)
cited_competitors.context: Used as precedent for cereblon‑recruiting glues targeting zinc‑finger proteins

unresolved_questions: Can ZBTB11 degraders be optimized for oral dosing and systemic exposure?
Will chronic ZBTB11 degradation affect normal tissue homeostasis beyond neurons?
